summary: 2026-05-06 crosscheck P0-1/P0-A through P0-E plus selected P1/P2 implementation updated release closeout around a single canonical batch manifest authority, optional audit-pack materialization, learning-delta closeout sequencing, explicit ZIP timestamp semantics, direct dashboard/clean-blocker ledger learning-guard display, typed same-eval telemetry consumption, proposal-family coverage selection, stateful learning-delta evidence coverage, subprocess timeout reason taxonomy, accepted-risk count source reconciliation, test-summary toolchain eligibility, and a machine-readable distribution profile matrix.

observations.surface: distribution packaging, external report manifest, release evidence artifacts
observations.problem: The source ZIP is clean and matches the source manifest, and local release evidence artifacts exist, but there is still no sealed companion bundle or attestation that binds the source ZIP SHA to the generated evidence set.
observations.why_it_matters: Without an explicit bundle boundary, a clean source package can be mistaken for a clean machine release even though ops/reports and external-reports are intentionally out of band.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a release-evidence-bundle target that packages selected evidence artifacts plus a distribution attestation containing source ZIP SHA, evidence bundle SHA, source manifest digest, policy digest, and archive profile.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06 by replacing the proposed mandatory companion bundle with release-closeout-batch-manifest as the canonical checked-in evidence authority, optional release-audit-pack materialization, and distribution ZIP path/content parity fields.

observations.surface: Makefile release-evidence-closeout, batch manifest, dashboard, clean blocker ledger
observations.problem: ops/reports/learning-delta-scoreboard.json exists, but release-evidence-closeout does not invoke learning-delta-scoreboard and the batch manifest, cohort, dashboard, and clean blocker ledger do not consume its guard status.
observations.why_it_matters: A schema-backed scoreboard that is generated outside the closeout chain remains advisory and can drift away from the release decision snapshot.
observations.automation_candidate: Wire learning-delta-scoreboard into release-evidence-closeout before release dashboards and include its digest and guard status in batch, dashboard, and clean-blocker summaries.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: release-evidence-closeout invokes learning-delta-scoreboard before release dashboards, the batch manifest seals the scoreboard digest, and release-evidence-dashboard plus release-clean-blocker-ledger now expose learning_claim_guard_status, learning_claim_allowed, same-eval reason coverage, behavior-delta digest coverage, and placeholder audit status.

observations.surface: release closeout batch manifest and artifact freshness ZIP metadata mode
observations.problem: The current ZIP has all member timestamps normalized to 1980-01-01, yet source_evidence_freshness currently records zip_member_timestamp as a pass basis without explicit timestamp semantics or source freshness authority fields.
observations.why_it_matters: ZIP member timestamps prevent fresh-extract filesystem mtime false positives, but normalized archive timestamps should not be read as temporal proof that source content is fresh.
observations.automation_candidate: Add timestamp_semantics, source_freshness_temporal_authority, and source_freshness_content_authority fields, backed by source-tree fingerprint or path/content digest parity.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: batch manifest now records timestamp_semantics, source_freshness_temporal_authority, source_freshness_content_authority, and distribution ZIP path/content parity so normalized 1980 ZIP timestamps are not treated as temporal authority.

observations.surface: auto-improve readiness, outcome metrics, mutation proposal queue, run telemetry schema
observations.problem: Run telemetry writers persist observed_at, proposal_id, source_candidate_id, behavior_delta_digest, and same_eval_reason, but readiness still does not directly consume those fields as typed same-eval evidence.
observations.why_it_matters: Free-text or writer-only telemetry leaves repeated same-eval learning claims review-heavy and can allow unknown/no-evidence states to masquerade as improvement readiness.
observations.automation_candidate: Introduce same_eval_reason_code, strict_secondary_improvement_present, secondary_improvement_axes, and proposal-family coverage selectors in readiness and scoreboard consumers.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: run telemetry schema/writer now emit same_eval_reason_code, strict_secondary_improvement_present, and secondary_improvement_axes; auto-improve readiness and learning-delta-scoreboard consume typed same-eval evidence; scoreboard uses stateful coverage and proposal-family coverage selection before allowing machine-checkable learning claims.

observations.surface: command runtime, test execution summary, release smoke subprocess diagnostics
observations.problem: TimeoutExpired handling can conflate true execution timeouts with process-output or signal-race recovery cases.
observations.why_it_matters: A false timeout can make non-hermetic subprocess diagnostics look like release-quality failures and obscures whether the process was still running, produced output, or recovered before signaling.
observations.automation_candidate: Expose startup_timeout, execution_timeout, timeout_after_output, and signal_race_recovered termination reasons while retaining timed_out and final_state_observed fields.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: command_runtime now classifies timeout termination_reason into execution_timeout, timeout_after_output, startup_timeout, or signal_race_recovered, with focused command-runtime tests covering the grace drain paths.

observations.surface: operator release summary and accepted-risk release authority counts
observations.problem: Operator, clean-lane, dashboard-attention, release-blocking, and advisory accepted-risk counts are different aggregation concepts but can appear as contradictory totals without explicit source and scope metadata.
observations.why_it_matters: Operators need to distinguish total accepted-risk families from clean-lane-blocking families and dashboard gate attention counts before treating a conditional release as machine-clean.
observations.automation_candidate: Attach source_artifact, source_path, field_path, aggregation_rule, included_scopes, and excluded_scopes metadata to accepted-risk count fields.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: operator-release-summary accepted_risk now includes count_sources for operator_accepted_risk_family_count, clean_lane_blocking_accepted_risk_family_count, accepted_risk_count, gate_attention_count, learning_claim_blocking_family_count, and advisory_lifecycle_family_count; dashboard/batch/operator reports separate actual accepted risk instances from non-risk gate attention and removed the legacy accepted_risk_gate_attention_count compatibility field.

observations.surface: test execution summary and full-suite evidence promotion
observations.problem: Test execution summaries recorded Python and pytest versions but did not state whether that toolchain was eligible to promote full-suite release evidence.
observations.why_it_matters: A full-suite artifact produced by an unsupported Python or pytest combination should not be silently treated as release-builder evidence.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a toolchain_contract with supported Python majors, minimum pytest major, pass/unsupported status, and full-suite promotion effect.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: test-execution-summary execution_environment now includes toolchain_contract and blocks full-suite evidence promotion when the toolchain is unsupported.

observations.surface: distribution packaging policy, evidence bundle lifecycle, public mirror boundary
observations.problem: Source content packages, release evidence bundles, public code mirrors, review snapshots, and local workspaces have different include/exclude rules but lacked one machine-readable matrix.
observations.why_it_matters: Without a shared profile matrix, generated artifacts, tmp diagnostics, private corpus surfaces, and evidence attestations can be mixed across source, evidence, public, and review package claims.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a schema-backed distribution-profile matrix covering included surfaces, excluded surfaces, generated artifact policy, tmp policy, and attestation role per profile.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: ops/policies/distribution-profile-matrix.json and its schema/test now define source_content_package, release_evidence_bundle, public_code_mirror, review_full_snapshot, and local_workspace profiles.

observations.surface: Makefile release-evidence-closeout, bootstrap/raw-registry preflight, generated report finalization
observations.problem: release-evidence-closeout could enter full-suite and closeout summary phases with stale bootstrap/raw-registry/generated report evidence after source-tree changes, producing source_tree_coherence_attention or self-check failures before the chain could regenerate the stale artifacts.
observations.why_it_matters: A closeout chain that cannot refresh its own freshness evidence forces manual repair loops and makes source-tree coherence look like an accepted policy risk instead of a deterministic sequencing property.
observations.automation_candidate: Regenerate bootstrap and raw-registry evidence adjacent to each other, pre-warm generated-index and report-contract summary evidence before full-suite self-checks, then rerun strict summaries after full-suite evidence is current.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: release-evidence-closeout now runs registry-preflight immediately after bootstrap-preflight, pre-warms generated-artifact-index/archive evidence, uses test-execution-summary-report-contract-refresh to break stale summary self-check loops, and then reruns strict full-suite plus report-contract summaries before final closeout artifacts.

observations.surface: release closeout summary, release clean blocker ledger, learning delta scoreboard
observations.problem: learning_blocked_by_review_required was still represented as a source clean-lane concern even when the release was not claiming a machine learning improvement.
observations.why_it_matters: Source releases should remain auditable and machine-clean when they do not claim learning improvement, while machine learning claims must still be blocked unless learning_claim_guard and learning readiness evidence allow the claim.
observations.automation_candidate: Make release closeout consume learning-delta-scoreboard claim context, skip learning_blocked_by_review_required for non-learning source releases, and keep learning signoff risks out of the clean-lane blocker ledger.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: release closeout now reads learning-delta-scoreboard before source aggregation, learning_blocked_by_review_required only gates closeout when claims_learning_improved=true, and release-clean-blocker-ledger excludes learning-readiness signoff risk from clean-lane blockers while preserving learning_claim_guard attention.

observations.surface: release closeout summary, release clean blocker ledger, release lane summary, batch manifest
observations.problem: Accepted-risk handling mixed source clean-lane blockers, operator-reviewed conditional risks, learning-claim blockers, and advisory lifecycle backlog into overlapping counts.
observations.why_it_matters: Without lane-scoped effects, advisory archive backlog or learning-review risk can be mistaken for source package impurity, while operators lose a clear audit trail for why a machine-clean release is or is not allowed.
observations.automation_candidate: Use a schema-backed release-risk-taxonomy policy as the single source of truth, annotate every closeout risk with lane effects, and derive clean, conditional, learning, and advisory counts from those effects.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-06: added ops/policies/release-risk-taxonomy.json and schema, annotated closeout blockers/accepted risks with lane effects, moved generated_index_archive_advisory to advisory_backlog, kept learning_blocked_by_review_required on the learning claim lane, exposed learning/advisory counts through cohort/lane/batch reports, added taxonomy coverage guard blocker release_risk_taxonomy_unregistered_code for unclassified closeout risk codes, removed legacy *_risk_count family-count aliases from schema/producer/tests/samples/reports, added release-risk-taxonomy-matrix JSON/Markdown generation for audit review, and added a pre-freshness closeout-shape refresh to release-evidence-closeout.
